dimensions.rationale: Receipt5 is bound to the requested baseline/candidate/tree and declares LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY, protected/production execution false, and zero remote writes; this reviewer performed only local static checks.

Commit/tree and supplied request/receipt hashes match exactly; receipt5 has 15 unique zero-exit commands with verified sanitized-output digests, and the provenance validator preserves six ordered attempts, distinct task IDs, historical hashes, final reply closure, and Git-bound drift checks.

Non-PASS or malformed assurance selects PRE_CLOSURE; v1.0.5 additionally requires deterministic rebuilding and clean Git-bound provenance. The state schema couples both permitted versions to exact blockers/findings, while protected, production, publication, and final acceptance remain blocked.

The immutable candidate is a clean detached Git boundary; final-output integration is blocked if candidate ancestry, tree identity, worktree cleanliness, or protected workflow/runtime/schema/test/assurance surfaces drift after review.

findings.finding: The original production-run capacity and Kill-control closure remains preserved as resolved history.
findings.resolution_ref: The retained Terra history records the capacity-gated, Kill-controlled resolution and the six-attempt validator requires complete history closure.

findings.finding: Post-review integrity closure remains resolved without permitting protected-surface mutation.
findings.resolution_ref: Protected prefixes and adversarial Git regressions reject workflow, delivery-authority, validation, runtime, dependency, schema, and test drift.

findings.finding: The delivery-state transition no longer permits crossed pre-closure and closed facts.
findings.resolution_ref: The schema has exactly two coupled alternatives for v1.0.4 pre-closure and v1.0.5 closed state, with explicit crossed-state negative checks.

findings.finding: Closed delivery state remains dependent on complete two-family provenance and clean Git-bound post-review integrity.
findings.resolution_ref: Only PASS assurance selects CLOSED; validation independently requires PASS assurance with candidate/tree ancestry, clean worktree, protected-path integrity, distinct replies, and full finding-history closure.

findings.finding: The shared evidence validator now routes Stage 6 v1 and candidate-bound v2 records exclusively by declared schema version, repairing the output-integration rejection without accepting unsupported versions.
findings.resolution_ref: The explicit two-schema map selects v1 or v2 only; unsupported versions return an error, while the dedicated Stage 6 validator binds v2 task and aggregate outputs to receipt5, candidate subject, command IDs, and local-only counters.

limitations: This static local review does not independently replay raw command logs or prove protected Oracle behavior, real Gmail/private-repository behavior, production health, deployment, publication, RMD-06, RMD-07, or final AC-033. Receipt5 intentionally retains no raw logs; platform task IDs remain auditable through the retained Codex thread audit log rather than signed third-party attestation.
summary: PASS: the immutable candidate and supplied candidate-bound receipt match the requested target; preserved five-attempt history is hash-consistent, the v1/v2 shared-validator repair is fail-closed, and the candidate can validate the planned final provenance wrapper without promoting protected or production claims.
